content:
Hello, I've been trying to apply and test the rust kcfi patch v2 but I keep running into errors wrt Kbuild recursive dependencies (and sometimes git am throws a fit and won't apply patch 2/2 without some manual intervention within init/Kconfig but that may be another error). My current environment to build and test is:

  • clang 18.1.8 (9)
  • rust 1.80.0
  • start applying patches on top of the v6.11-rc2 tag in Linus's tree
  • based on x86_64_defconfig with lkdtm = y and cfi_clang=yset from menuconfig
  • compiling with make LLVM=1 CONFIG_RUST=y CONFIG_CFI_ICALL_NORMALIZE_INTEGERS=y (enabling rust clang_cfi in menuconfig disables rust from appearing in menuconfig)

error is:

error: recursive dependency detected!
        symbol RUST depends on RUSTC_VERSION
        symbol RUSTC_VERSION depends on RUST
For a resolution refer to Documentation/kbuild/kconfig-language.rst
subsection "Kconfig recursive dependency limitations"

I have a feeling this is just something small I'm missing (perhaps I need to start from rust-next or another branch/tree/tag instead of v6.11-rc2?)
Miguel hasn't sent the new version of RUSTC_VERSION yet, but you can find it here: https://github.com/ojeda/linux/commits/rust-rustc-version/
You're welcome. Happy to hear that you're trying it out :)

lkdtm looks good with CFI_FORWARD_PROTO based on Miguel's branch with the rust kcfi patches applied! I had to switch to nightly for some unstable features but otherwise not too difficult.

afaik, I should wait for the new RUSTC_VERSION patch before adding a Tested-By tag (and I'll have to retest with the new RUSTC_VERSION anyways)

either way, excited by the work here!
There was an unstable feature warning/error I received during compilation, perhaps nightly wasn't strictly necessary to use it. Can't remember specifically what feature
Sounds familiar. Is there a way to enable it on 1.80?
